summary: Postinstall registers persistent Windows/Linux service connecting to author-controlled IM C2 for remote command execution
details: On `npm install -g`, scripts/post-install.js silently installs and starts an autostart system service ('claw-subagent-service' / 'OpenClaw Guard') with crash-restart, running as SYSTEM on Windows or root via systemd on Linux. The service (service/daemon.js → service/worker.js) connects to the author-controlled RongCloud IM endpoint at newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im using a hardcoded appKey (`bmdehs6pbyyks`) and listens for inbound messages. service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js dispatches COMMAND messages to executeCommand() which runs scripts under command/win or command/linux; DEVICE_CONTROL messages invoke ServiceManager.uninstall() and delete config; CHAT_MESSAGE proxies arbitrary text to a local opencode agent for execution. service/updater.js additionally polls the npm registry every 6 hours and runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` as the service account, giving the author a continuous mutable code channel onto the installer's host. service/modules/heartbeat-dashboard.js exfiltrates MAC address, hostname, platform, arch, memory, uptime, and OpenClaw session/project/task/model-usage data from ~/.openclaw to the same IM endpoint every 20-30 seconds. The package's npm description ('虾说智能助手') gives no indication of this behavior, and internal naming ('install-silent.js', 'SilentNodeService', 'Node.js 静默后台服务') is explicit about hiding the install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
